subject: Mathematics
title: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables

questions.text: The general form of a linear equation in two variables x and y is:
questions.explanation: A linear equation in two variables has the highest power of variables as 1.

questions.text: If a₁/a₂ ≠ b₁/b₂, the pair of linear equations has:
questions.explanation: When the coefficients are not proportional, the lines intersect at exactly one point.

questions.text: If a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂ ≠ c₁/c₂, the lines represented by the equations are:
questions.explanation: Parallel lines have the same slope but different intercepts, resulting in no common solution.

questions.text: If a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂ = c₁/c₂, the system of equations is:
questions.explanation: When all ratios are equal, the lines coincide, giving infinitely many solutions.

questions.text: A pair of linear equations which has no solution is called:
questions.explanation: Inconsistent systems refer to parallel lines that never meet.

questions.text: The graphical representation of x = 3 is a line parallel to:
questions.explanation: x = constant is always a vertical line parallel to the y-axis.

questions.text: The graphical representation of y = -2 is a line parallel to:
questions.explanation: y = constant is a horizontal line parallel to the x-axis.

questions.text: The solution of x + y = 10 and x - y = 2 is:
questions.explanation: Adding equations: 2x = 12 => x=6. Then 6+y=10 => y=4.

questions.text: If 2x + 3y = 12, then the point where it cuts the x-axis is:
For what value of k will the equations x + 2y = 3 and 5x + ky + 7 = 0 be parallel?
questions.explanation: Condition for parallel: a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂. 1/5 = 2/k => k = 10.

questions.text: A consistent system of equations must have at least:
questions.explanation: Consistency implies the lines meet at one or more points.

questions.text: The point of intersection of x = a and y = b is:
questions.explanation: The lines meet where the x-coordinate is 'a' and y-coordinate is 'b'.

questions.text: The pair of equations y = 0 and y = -7 has:
questions.explanation: These are two distinct horizontal lines which never intersect.

questions.text: The area of the triangle formed by the lines x = 3, y = 4 and x = y is:
questions.explanation: Points are (3,3), (4,4) and (3,4). Area = 1/2 * base * height = 1/2 * 1 * 1 = 0.5.

questions.text: Find k if kx + 3y = k - 3 and 12x + ky = k have infinitely many solutions.
questions.explanation: k/12 = 3/k = (k-3)/k. From k²=36, k=±6. k=6 satisfies the third part (6-3)/6 = 1/2.

questions.text: The value of y when x = 5 in the equation 3x - 2y = 7 is:
If x=a, y=b is the solution of x-y=2 and x+y=4, then a and b are:
questions.explanation: Adding: 2x = 6 => a=3. Subtracting: 2y = 2 => b=1.

questions.text: Method of solving linear equations by eliminating one variable is called:
questions.explanation: Elimination involves making coefficients of one variable equal to remove it.

questions.text: In the substitution method, we express one variable in terms of:
questions.explanation: We isolate one variable and plug its expression into the second equation.

questions.text: If 2x + 3y = 11 and 2x - 4y = -24, find the value of 'm' for which y = mx + 3.
questions.explanation: Solving equations gives x = -2, y = 5. 5 = m(-2) + 3 => 2 = -2m => m = -1.

questions.text: The sum of two numbers is 35 and their difference is 13. The numbers are:
A pair of linear equations representing coincident lines is always:
questions.explanation: Since they share every point, they have infinite solutions and are consistent.

questions.text: Aruna has only Re 1 and Rs 2 coins with her. If total coins are 50 and amount is Rs 75, then number of Rs 1 and Rs 2 coins are:
questions.explanation: x+y=50, x+2y=75. Subtracting gives y=25, so x=25.

questions.text: If the lines given by 3x + 2ky = 2 and 2x + 5y + 1 = 0 are parallel, then k is:
The system x - y = 1 and x + y = 3 has:
questions.explanation: Adding: 2x = 4 => x=2. Then 2-y=1 => y=1.

questions.text: Which method was recently removed from the Grade 10 NCERT syllabus for solving linear equations?
questions.explanation: The cross-multiplication method was removed in the recent rationalization of the syllabus.

questions.text: One equation of a pair of dependent linear equations is -5x + 7y = 2. The second could be:
questions.explanation: Multiplying the first equation by -2 gives 10x - 14y = -4.

questions.text: For what value of 'p' will the system 2x + 3y = 7 and px + 6y = 12 have no solution?
The father's age is six times his son's age. Four years hence, it is four times. Their present ages are:
The value of c for which the pair cx - y = 2 and 6x - 2y = 3 will have no solution is:
Intersecting lines represent which type of system?
questions.explanation: Consistent systems have at least one solution (intersecting or coincident).

questions.text: If x=2, y=3 is a solution of 2x + 3y = k, then k is:
The coordinates of the origin are:
questions.explanation: The origin is the point where the x and y axes intersect.

questions.text: A line parallel to the x-axis is of the form:
questions.explanation: Horizontal lines have a constant y-value.

questions.text: A line parallel to the y-axis is of the form:
questions.explanation: Vertical lines have a constant x-value.

questions.text: If two lines are coincident, how many solutions are there?
questions.explanation: Coincident lines overlap completely, sharing every point as a solution.

questions.text: The pair of equations x + 2y + 5 = 0 and -3x - 6y + 1 = 0 have:
questions.explanation: 1/-3 = 2/-6 ≠ 5/1. Since a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂ ≠ c₁/c₂, there is no solution.

questions.text: The lines x = 2 and y = 3 are:
questions.explanation: One is vertical and one is horizontal; they intersect at (2, 3).

questions.text: If a pair of linear equations is consistent, then the lines will be:
questions.explanation: Consistency means having one or more solutions.

questions.text: If a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂ = c₁/c₂ then the system is called ________ consistent.
questions.explanation: Coincident lines are referred to as dependent consistent systems.
